Scratch — RRS A8.1 cannot separate, so RRS A8.2 last-race countback decides

RRS A8.1 then A8.2. Alice and Bob each score 8 points with identical sorted score lists ([1, 2, 5] each), so A8.1 cannot separate them. A8.2 then ranks by the last race (both DNC, equal), then the next-to-last race (race 2), where Bob finished 1st and Alice 2nd: Bob ranks ahead. These countback scores are used even though one of them (the DNC) would be discarded — A8.2 includes excluded scores.

Scoring configuration: No discards. DNF/OCS scoring: A5.2 (series entries).

4 boats, N+1 = 5.

Alice: 1st, 2nd, DNC → 1+2+5=8  sorted best-to-worst [1, 2, 5]
Bob:   2nd, 1st, DNC → 2+1+5=8  sorted best-to-worst [1, 2, 5] ← identical to Alice
Carol: 3rd, 3rd, 1st → 3+3+1=7  (wins outright)
David: 4th, 4th, 2nd → 4+4+2=10

Tie-break (Alice vs Bob):
  A8.1 — sorted scores [1,2,5] vs [1,2,5] → identical, cannot separate
  A8.2 — last race (race 3): Alice=5, Bob=5 → equal
         next-to-last race (race 2): Alice=2, Bob=1 → Bob wins
    Alice and Bob both DNC; Carol wins, David 2nd
Rank Sail Number Boat Competitor R1 R2 R3 Total
1st 303 Carol 3.0 3.0 1.0 7.0
2nd 202 Bob 2.0 1.0 5.0 DNC 8.0
3rd 101 Alice 1.0 2.0 5.0 DNC 8.0
4th 404 David 4.0 4.0 2.0 10.0

R1 — 1 Jan 2025

Rank Sail Number Boat Competitor Points
1 101 Alice 1.0
2 202 Bob 2.0
3 303 Carol 3.0
4 404 David 4.0

R2 — 1 Jan 2025

Rank Sail Number Boat Competitor Points
1 202 Bob 1.0
2 101 Alice 2.0
3 303 Carol 3.0
4 404 David 4.0

R3 — 1 Jan 2025

Rank Sail Number Boat Competitor Points
1 303 Carol 1.0
2 404 David 2.0
101 Alice 5.0 DNC
202 Bob 5.0 DNC

Sail Scoring — sailscoring.ie —
